name: Chocolate Bread Pudding with Cherry Raspberry Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup butter or margarine, cut into pieces
16 ounces pitted dark sweet cherries, drained (reserve liquid)
10 ounces frozen raspberries, thawed and drained (reserve liquid)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Grease a 12 x 8-inch (2 quart) baking dish.
3. In a large saucepan, combine chocolate chips and whipping cream. Heat over medium-low heat until the chocolate is melted, stirring occasionally. Stir in 1/3 cup of the brown sugar.
4. Add egg yolks one at a time, blending well after each addition. Continue cooking until slightly thickened, stirring constantly.
5. Add margarine and vanilla; stir until smooth. Remove from heat and stir in bread cubes.
6. In a large bowl, beat egg whites at medium speed until soft peaks form.
7. Gradually add the remaining 1/3 cup brown sugar, beating at high speed until stiff peaks form. Fold egg white mixture into chocolate mixture.
8. Pour into the greased baking dish. Place in a larger pan (13 x 9-inch or larger).
9. Bake for 40 minutes or until the center is set.
10. In a medium saucepan, combine sugar and cornstarch. Gradually stir in the reserved liquids from the fruits. Cook over medium-high heat until the mixture thickens and forms a soft, shiny coating (bowl), stirring constantly.
11. Cool slightly; stir in fruit.
12. Serve warm bread pudding with Cherry Raspberry Sauce. Store any remaining bread pudding and sauce in the refrigerator.
